title: विष्णु हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु हृदयम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
शान्ताकारं भुजगशयनं पद्मनाभं सुरेशं
विश्वाधारं गगनसदृशं मेघवर्णं शुभाङ्गम्।
लक्ष्मीकान्तं कमलनयनं योगिभिर्ध्यानगम्यं
वन्दे विष्णुं भवभयहरं सर्वलोकैकनाथम्॥

॥ हृदयम् ॥
विष्णोर्हृदयं देवस्य सर्वलोकैकपालकम्।
नमस्ये परमात्मानं वासुदेवं जनार्दनम्॥ १॥

यो विष्णुः सर्वभूतेषु हृदये सर्वदा स्थितः।
तस्मै नमो भगवते विष्णवे सर्वसाक्षिणे॥ २॥

चतुर्भुजं महाविष्णुं शङ्खचक्रगदाधरम्।
पीताम्बरधरं देवं नमामि पुरुषोत्तमम्॥ ३॥

नारायणं महादेवं सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम्।
क्षीराब्धिशयनं विष्णुं हृदये सततं भजे॥ ४॥

त्वमेव सृष्टिकर्ता च त्वमेव परिपालकः।
त्वमेव संहर्ता चापि त्वमेव परमेश्वरः॥ ५॥

त्वमादिदेवो भगवान् प्रपन्नार्तिहरो हरिः।
त्वं पिता सर्वलोकानां त्वं माता त्वं गुरुः परः॥ ६॥

सर्वव्याधिहरं दिव्यं सर्वसम्पत्तिवर्धनम्।
विष्णोर्हृदयमेतद्धि पठेद्यः शृणुयाद्वापि॥ ७॥

सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति।
मुक्तिदं सर्वदुःखघ्नं विष्णुहृदयमुत्तमम्॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री विष्णु हृदयं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Hridayam ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Shantakaram Bhujagashayanam Padmanabham Suresham
Vishvadharam Gaganasadrisham Meghavarnam Shubhangam.
Lakshmikantam Kamalanayanam Yogibhirdhyanagamyam
Vande Vishnum Bhavabhayaharam Sarvalokaikanatham.

|| Hridayam ||
Vishnor-hridayam Devasya Sarvalokaika-palakam.
Namasye Paramatmanam Vasudevam Janardanam. || 1 ||

Yo Vishnuh Sarvabhuteshu Hridaye Sarvada Sthitah.
Tasmai Namo Bhagavate Vishnave Sarvasakshine. || 2 ||

Chaturbhujam Mahavishnum Shankhachakragadadharam.
Pitambaradharam Devam Namami Purushottamam. || 3 ||

Narayanam Mahadevam Sarvalokmaheshvaram.
Kshirabdhishayanam Vishnum Hridaye Satatam Bhaje. || 4 ||

Tvameva Srishtikarta Cha Tvameva Paripalakah.
Tvameva Samharta Chapi Tvameva Parameshvarah. || 5 ||

Tvamadidevo Bhagavan Prapannartiharo Harih.
Tvam Pita Sarvalokaanam Tvam Mata Tvam Guruh Parah. || 6 ||

Sarvavyadhiharam Divyam Sarvasampattivaardhanam.
Vishnor-hridayametaddhi Pathedyah Shrinuyadvaapi. || 7 ||

Sarvapaapavinirmuktau Vishnulokam Sa Gachchhati.
Muktidam Sarvaduhkhghnam Vishnu-Hridayamuttamam. || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Hridayam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: यह स्तोत्र भगवान विष्णु के हृदय (सार तत्व) का वर्णन करता है। शान्त स्वरूप, शेषशय्या पर शयन करने वाले, पद्मनाभ, सुरेश्वर, विश्वाधार, मेघवर्ण, लक्ष्मीकान्त, कमलनयन विष्णु को ध्यान में रखते हुए उनकी स्तुति की गई है। भगवान विष्णु सभी प्राणियों के हृदय में सर्वदा विराजमान हैं, वे सृष्टिकर्ता, पालक और संहारकर्ता हैं। वे समस्त लोकों के पिता, माता और परम गुरु हैं। जो इस हृदय स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है वह सभी पापों से मुक्त होकर विष्णुलोक को प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Invokes the supreme blessings of lord vishnu
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases and bestows divine health
Traditionally associated with wealth, prosperity, and all-round success
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and leads to liberation (moksha)
Invokes the blessing of peace, happiness, and spiritual advancement
description: Vishnu Hridayam — विष्णु हृदयम् — is a heart-essence prayer offered to Lord Vishnu. This revered text originates from Narada Purana. Regular recitation is believed to destroy all sins and leads to liberation (moksha), grant the supreme blessings of Lord Vishnu, and bring wealth, prosperity, and all-round success. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Vaikuntha Ekadashi and Vishnu Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 7 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vishnu Hridayam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Sacred hymn to Lord Vishnu for liberation, prosperity, and divine grace.

Vishnu Hridayam meaning heart of Vishnu

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vishnu Hridayam and what does the title mean?
seoFaqs.answer: Vishnu Hridayam — literally the heart of Vishnu — is a sacred stotra that, in the Vaishnavite tradition, is regarded as containing the essence (hridaya, heart) of Lord Vishnu's divine nature and protective power. The word hridayam in stotra titles signifies a hymn that distils the supreme truth or essence of a deity, much as the Aditya Hridayam (taught by Agastya to Rama) distils the essence of Surya worship. The Vishnu Hridayam similarly distils the protective and liberative power of Vishnu into a focused recitation, considered especially efficacious for those seeking refuge in the Lord during periods of crisis, illness or spiritual longing. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia Vaishnavism; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Vishnu Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Hridayam is preserved in the Narada Purana, one of the eighteen Mahapuranas of Vaishnavite orientation associated with sage Narada. The Narada Purana contains extensive Vaishnava bhakti material — descriptions of pilgrimage centres, vrata observances, mantra-shastra and stotras — and the Vishnu Hridayam appears within its sections devoted to Vishnu upasana. Compilation texts such as Brihat Stotra Ratnakara, the stotra-mala used by Sri Vaishnava and Madhva temple priests, and major regional pothi prints reproduce the hymn from this source. The hymn is widely circulated through Stotra Nidhi, Vedadhara, Sanskritdocuments.org and Gita Press editions in multiple Indian languages. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia Narada Purana; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Vishnu Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Thursday (Guruvar) is the universally accepted weekday for any Vishnu stotra. Daily morning recitation after the snana and sandhya is the standard practice. Ekadashi tithi of every fortnight is particularly auspicious, especially Vaikuntha Ekadashi in Margashirsha and Nirjala Ekadashi in Jyeshtha. Other peak occasions include Janmashtami, Rama Navami, Narasimha Jayanti, Vamana Jayanti, the Chaturmasya period, and any pilgrimage to Vaishnava tirthas like Tirumala, Srirangam, Puri or Badrinath. Devotees facing serious illness, terminal disease, planetary affliction, or seeking deep spiritual refuge are particularly advised to recite the Vishnu Hridayam daily as a prayer for divine grace. (Sources: Drikpanchang Vaikuntha Ekadashi; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Vishnu Hridayam phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narada Purana phalashruti of the Vishnu Hridayam promises that one who recites it daily with bhakti has all sins of the present life and previous births destroyed, attains the protection of Lord Vishnu, gains material prosperity and longevity, and ultimately reaches the supreme abode of Vaikuntha. Specific benefits include freedom from chronic disease, mental peace, removal of fear and anxiety, success in dharmic undertakings, harmony in family life, and progress on the path of bhakti-yoga. The hymn is particularly recommended for those facing serious crises — incurable disease, persecution, financial collapse, mental anguish — as a direct appeal to Vishnu's compassionate nature. Recitation on Ekadashi or during Chaturmasya is said to multiply the benefits. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Vedadhara; Hindupedia Vaishnavism.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Vishnu Hridayam different from the Aditya Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are hridayam stotras — distilled essences of devotion to a particular deity — but they address different objects of worship and serve different functions. The Aditya Hridayam is the famous hymn from the Valmiki Ramayana (Yuddha Kanda) taught by sage Agastya to Lord Rama before his battle with Ravana, addressed to Surya the sun god, and recited primarily for victory in battle, removal of sins, healing of eye disease, and physical strength. The Vishnu Hridayam, preserved in the Narada Purana, is addressed to Lord Vishnu the cosmic preserver, and recited primarily for spiritual refuge, freedom from sin, devotion (bhakti), and ultimate moksha in Vaikuntha. Devotees may recite both, the Aditya Hridayam at sunrise and Vishnu Hridayam at any time of day. (Sources: Hindupedia Aditya Hridayam; Stotra Nidhi; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: Can the Vishnu Hridayam be recited by anyone?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. As a Vaishnavite stotra preserved in the open puranic tradition of the Narada Purana, the Vishnu Hridayam is accessible to all devotees regardless of gender, caste, age or sampradaya affiliation, and no formal mantra-diksha is required for daily recitation. Children, women, householders and renunciates all recite the hymn as part of personal devotion. The only customary requirements are cleanliness of body and place, sitting facing east or north, and offering tulsi leaves, yellow flowers, sandalwood paste and naivedya before a Vishnu image, Shaligram or Lakshmi-Narayana murti. The recitation is best preceded by the Vishnu dhyana shloka Shantakaram Bhujagashayanam Padmanabham Suresham. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What offerings traditionally accompany the Vishnu Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional Vaishnava offerings used during Vishnu Hridayam recitation are tulsi leaves and tulsi-mala — most beloved to Vishnu and considered indispensable to any Vishnu puja — along with yellow or white flowers (especially lotus, parijata, chameli), yellow chandan (sandalwood paste), turmeric, kumkum, and naivedya of fruits, milk-sweets like kheer or rice-payasam, panakam (jaggery water in summer) and tulsi-charanamrit. Devotees light a ghee lamp or sesame-oil lamp before a Vishnu image, Shaligram or Lakshmi-Narayana murti, often facing east or northeast. Wearing yellow or white clothing is considered auspicious, and the recitation is typically followed by namaskara prostrations and parikrama (circumambulation) of the altar. (Sources: Hindupedia Vaishnava puja; Drikpanchang; Stotra Nidhi.)
